message:
Add EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS for aggregating synced remote machines

Users who already sync ~/.claude between machines (via rsync, Syncthing,
etc.) can now aggregate usage from all of them into a single report
without installing the client on every machine. Set EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS
to a comma-separated list of directories, each containing a projects/
subdirectory of Claude Code JSONL sessions, and the reporter will run
ccusage once per directory (via CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) and merge the results.

Running ccusage separately per config dir — rather than pointing a
single ccusage at all of them via a comma-joined CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR —
avoids OOM when the combined session history is large. Each subprocess
gets its own heap with --max-old-space-size=8192 for headroom.

Error handling degrades gracefully: if the local ccusage succeeds but a
remote dir fails (or vice versa), the successful data is still reported.
Only if every ccusage call fails does Claude collection count as failed.

Authored by Claude Code (Opus 4.6) at Jesse Vincent's direction.

files.patch:
@@ -37,3 +37,10 @@ DEMO_VIDEO_URL=
 # This is shown on your profile page so others can see your setup.
 # We NEVER send prompts, responses, conversation history, file contents, or API keys.
 REPORT_MACHINE_CONFIG=true
+# Aggregate Claude Code usage from additional machines without installing the
+# client on each. Comma-separated list of directories; each must contain a
+# `projects/` subdirectory of JSONL session files (i.e. the same layout as
+# ~/.claude). Point this at a synced snapshot of a remote machine's ~/.claude
+# and its usage will be collected alongside local usage on every report.
+# Example: EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS=/path/to/synced-laptop,/path/to/synced-desktop
+# EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS=

@@ -144,6 +144,16 @@ The client supports reporting from multiple machines under the same username. Ea
 
 Your [profile page](https://tokenmaxxing.odio.dev) shows how many machines you're reporting from.
 
+### Aggregating from synced remote machines
+
+If you already sync `~/.claude` from other machines to a central location (e.g. via rsync, Syncthing, or a tool like [engineering-notebook](https://github.com/obra/engineering-notebook)), you can aggregate all of them into a single report without installing the client on each machine. Set `EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS` in `.env` to a comma-separated list of directories, each containing a `projects/` subdirectory of Claude Code JSONL sessions:
+
+```
+EXTRA_CLAUDE_CONFIGS=/path/to/synced-laptop,/path/to/synced-desktop
+```
+
+The reporter runs `ccusage` once per directory (using `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR`) and merges the results with the local machine's usage before submitting.
+
 ## Profile Page
 
 Each user gets a shareable profile at `https://tokenmaxxing.odio.dev/user/YOUR_NAME` showing:
